sprints.title: The Autopilot v2 — Smart Model Routing

tickets.title: Model success rate tracking — per-club, per-ticket-type breakdown
tickets.description: Extend slope-loop/model-selector.ts (NOT analyze-scorecards.ts) to compute success rates per-club-per-strategy and per-club-per-sprint-type from JSONL result files. Add configurable min_samples threshold (default >= 3). Update ModelConfig interface. Fix existing type inconsistency: recommendations.model typed as 'api'|'local' in types.ts but written as plain string in model-selector.ts.
acceptance_criteria: slope-loop/model-selector.ts outputs per-club-per-strategy success rates in model-config.json
slope-loop/model-selector.ts outputs per-club-per-sprint-type success rates in model-config.json
Recommendations only emitted when sample count >= min_samples for a dimension
ModelConfig.recommendations.model type inconsistency fixed (aligned between types.ts and model-selector.ts)
Existing ModelConfig consumers (selectModel, loadModelConfig) still work without changes
Tests cover fallback when most cross-dimensional cells are empty (cold start)

tickets.title: Adaptive model selection — apply cross-dimensional recommendations
tickets.description: Extend selectModel() to accept optional sprintType (BacklogSprint['type']) and wire existing strategy param into recommendation lookup. Check cross-dimensional recommendations: club+sprintType > club+strategy > club-only > defaults. Only apply override if sample count meets min_samples. Note: type/strategy are sprint-level fields (not per-ticket).
acceptance_criteria: selectModel() accepts optional sprintType parameter (BacklogSprint['type'])
Cross-dimensional lookup priority: club+sprintType > club+strategy > club-only > defaults
Recommendations with insufficient samples fall through to next tier
Existing callers without sprintType param still work unchanged
Tests cover full fallback chain including cold start (all cross-dimensional cells empty)

tickets.title: Model cost-performance tradeoff — factor cost into routing decisions
tickets.description: Add cost_adjusted_score = success_rate / (cost_per_success + epsilon) metric to slope-loop/model-selector.ts output. In src/cli/loop/model-selector.ts selectModel(), prefer the model with better cost-adjusted score when both have sufficient samples. Note: slope-loop/model-selector.ts has diverged significantly (own TicketResult interface, hardcoded model names) — treat as separate implementation.
acceptance_criteria: model-config.json includes cost_adjusted_scores per model per club
selectModel() uses cost-adjusted score when both models have sufficient data
slope-loop/model-selector.ts computes cost-adjusted scores in its own logic
Tests verify cost-adjusted routing preference

tickets.title: slope loop config recommend — surface optimal config from data
tickets.description: Add 'recommend' subcommand to slope loop config. Reads model-config.json, formats recommendations as a table (club > model > reason > samples > rate). Warns if model-config.json missing or stale (> 7 days). Add --json flag for machine-readable output.
acceptance_criteria: slope loop config recommend prints formatted recommendation table
Warns when model-config.json is missing or older than 7 days
--json flag outputs machine-readable JSON
Slots into existing loop config subcommand routing without disrupting --show/--set

tickets.title: Audit all advisory guards — classify mechanical vs advisory
tickets.description: Read all 29 guard files in src/cli/guards/ and classify by return shape: mechanical (blockReason/decision:'block' or disk write), advisory (context/additionalContext only), mixed. Add optional guardType field to GuardDefinition in guard.ts. Populate for all 28 entries in GUARD_DEFINITIONS. Use grep heuristic first, verify edge cases manually.
acceptance_criteria: Every guard in GUARD_DEFINITIONS has a guardType classification
guardType field is optional on GuardDefinition interface (backward compatible)
Classification matches actual guard behavior (verified by reading each guard file)
TypeScript compiles clean with the new field

tickets.title: Convert hazard guard to write disk state for compaction survival
tickets.description: After computing warnings, write to .slope/guard-state/hazard.json. On next invocation, read state file first — if warning for file+sprint exists on disk, include it regardless of context. Clear stale entries on sprint change. Create .slope/guard-state/ with mkdirSync recursive. Wrap disk writes in try/catch (fail open).
acceptance_criteria: hazard.ts writes warnings to .slope/guard-state/hazard.json after computing them
Previously-written warnings are restored from disk on subsequent invocations
Stale entries cleared when sprint number changes
.slope/guard-state/ created automatically if missing
Disk write failures don't crash the guard (fail open)
Tests cover: write+restore round-trip, sprint change clears state, missing directory creation

tickets.title: Convert scope-drift guard to write disk state for compaction survival
tickets.description: scope-drift returns { context } (advisory), NOT blockReason. After successful store query finding drift violation, write to .slope/guard-state/scope-drift.json. If store unavailable, read from state file as fallback. Return context (not blockReason) from disk — preserve advisory behavior. Auto-prune entries older than 7 days. If disk state > 24h old, fail-open.
acceptance_criteria: scope-drift.ts writes drift violations to .slope/guard-state/scope-drift.json
Store-unavailable path falls back to disk state — returns context (advisory), not blockReason
Stale entries cleared on sprint change or when store says file is in-scope
Entries older than 7 days auto-pruned on read
Disk state older than 24 hours treated as stale — fail-open
Tests cover: store-down fallback from disk, sprint change clears, in-scope clears state, staleness timeout

tickets.title: Guard enforcement report — slope guard audit or doctor check
tickets.description: Add 'slope guard audit' subcommand (not slope doctor — doctor checks are hardcoded, not a plugin system). Reads GUARD_DEFINITIONS, groups by guardType, prints table: name | type | event | description. Highlight advisory guards with compaction-loss warning. Wire into existing guard subcommand router alongside status/enable/disable.
acceptance_criteria: Command outputs table of all guards grouped by mechanical/advisory/mixed
Advisory guards shown with compaction-loss warning
Table includes guard name, type, event, and description
Tests verify output format and grouping
